Strategy Description: Exploits an overreaction to the well-known Post-Earnings Announcement Drift (PEAD) anomaly in liquid stocks.
Core Logic: Investors have started to overreact to earnings news in easy-to-arbitrage stocks. Stocks with the worst returns during the previous earnings announcement tend to outperform at the next one.
Entry Conditions: Long the decile of stocks with the lowest abnormal performance during the previous earnings announcement; short the decile with the highest.
Exit Conditions: Stocks are held for two days around the earnings announcement.
Indicators Used: Abnormal performance during the previous earnings announcement.
Risk Management Rules: Equally weighted portfolio; focus on large-cap stocks with active options markets.
Market Regime Suitability: Unknown; the long leg is correlated to the market, while the short leg may provide some hedging.
Full Notes: The strategy targets firms where investors are now overcompensating for historical underreaction to earnings news.
